problem_statement: Current AI hiring systems present humans with scores and recommendations that they cannot meaningfully interpret, enabling autonomous AI decisions and rubber-stamping that fail EU AI Act Article 14's meaningful human-oversight requirements for high-risk employment AI.
magnitude_evidence: In Mobley v. Workday, plaintiff applied to over 100 positions and was rejected for all; class certified May 16, 2025; rejections sent at 1:00 AM indicating no human review
insufficiency_evidence: "humans rubber-stamp AI decisions... they click 'Proceed' or 'Reject.' This is not human oversight. This is automation bias codified into a workflow"; explainability dashboards showing factor contributions are "not the same as enabling genuine interpretation"
solution_mechanisms: cross-vendor arbitrator to reduce single-source bias
human decision interface showing full reasoning
governance_refs: EU AI Act Annex III Point 4(a)
